forbid.label: Extension-UI check must compare origins, not substring-match a URL
forbid.advice: Use isExtensionFrontend(): a web page can put the extension id in its own URL, so substring matching is forgeable.

forbid.label: Do not derive trust from a substring/prefix test on sender.url/origin
forbid.advice: Compare parsed origins (isExtensionFrontend) — any substring/prefix API on sender.url/origin is forgeable.

forbid.label: Localhost gate must be an exact loopback host check, not a string test
forbid.advice: Use isSecureOrigin() (hostname === 'localhost'/'127.0.0.1'/'[::1]'); any string test admits http://localhost.evil.com.

require.label: Permission middleware calls the frontend trust gate
Background worker calls the frontend trust gate
Background worker enforces SELF_ONLY_REQUESTS for non-frontend callers
Frontend gate compares the exact extension origin
Frontend gate also checks sender.id against the extension id
